message:
feat(core,fuse,journal): add btrfs read/link/readlink guards, FUSE rename flags check, refactor JBD2 replay to event-ordered model

- Add symlink guard in ext4 readlink path and btrfs read/readlink paths,
  returning format errors for non-symlink readlink and non-file read
- Return EPERM instead of IsDirectory for btrfs hard-link of directories
- Reject non-zero FUSE rename flags (NOREPLACE/EXCHANGE) instead of
  silently ignoring them
- Add btrfs_write_setattr_extend_zero_fills and
  btrfs_write_setattr_same_size_is_noop tests
- Refactor JBD2 replay from separate writes/revoked sets to event-ordered
  PendingTxn body_events, preserving write-then-revoke ordering within
  a transaction
- Regenerate EBR benchmark artifact
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ivax","title":"ffs-cli dump group command reads full ext4 image","description":"dump_group_cmd currently uses load_ext4_reader (std::fs::read whole image) even though it only needs a single ext4 group descriptor and optional raw descriptor bytes. Refactor to bounded descriptor reads from disk.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:58:02.653478319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:59:52.868804566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:59:52.868778147Z","close_reason":"Completed: dump_group_cmd now uses ext4_superblock_for_action + read_ext4_group_desc_from_path to read/parse only the target descriptor window from disk (no full-image load). Shared helper is also used by build_ext4_group_info; added regression test read_ext4_group_desc_from_path_reads_single_descriptor_window and re-verified existing group-info test. Gates: fmt, targeted tests, check, clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","memory","reliability"]}
 {"id":"bd-j960","title":"Align README architecture/dependency claims with source manifests","description":"Source-level investigation found README drift against Cargo manifests: (1) README claims asupersync/ftui must be sibling checkouts, but workspace uses crates.io versions; (2) architecture section implies direct ffs-core -> ffs-repair write-path dependency, while manifests route repair through ffs-mvcc; (3) harness placement/dependency wording can be tightened to match actual crate graph. Update README to match current codebase architecture and dependency reality.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:40:32.586340068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T23:41:15.301461460Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T23:41:15.301443086Z","close_reason":"README now matches source manifests for dependency sourcing and repair/layering wording","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","docs","readme"]}
+{"id":"bd-jkm9","title":"Add btrfs setattr extend-zero-fills and same-size-noop tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:40:22.616980084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T02:40:25.881875626Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T02:40:25.881592941Z","close_reason":"Two new tests added and passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jtt2","title":"Implement external journal paired replay (not just UUID validation)","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 filesystems can use an external journal device (JOURNAL_DEV incompat flag on the data FS, journal_dev field points to device number, journal_uuid links the pair). When crash recovery is needed, the JBD2 journal blocks live on the external device rather than in an internal inode.

CURRENT STATE: OpenOptions::external_journal_path accepts an external journal image path. maybe_probe_external_journal opens it, reads its JBD2 superblock, validates UUID pairing and block size match, and stores ExternalJournalInfo on OpenFs. But maybe_replay_ext4_journal short-circuits when journal_dev != 0 — it logs 'journal replay skipped' and returns Ok(None). No actual replay occurs.

The log message honestly states: 'external journal UUID validation only; full paired replay not yet implemented'.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is Some AND UUIDs match:
   a. Open the external journal image as a ByteDevice.
   b. Parse the JBD2 superblock from the external device.
   c. Compute journal segments: the entire external device (minus superblock) is the journal area.
   d. Feed the segments to replay_jbd2_segments the same way we do for internal journals.
   e. The replay writes go to the DATA filesystem's device, not the journal device.
2. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery IS needed:
   - Currently returns UnsupportedFeature error (good — fail-fast).
   - No change needed here.
3. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery NOT needed:
   - Currently returns Ok(None) with info log (good — graceful skip).
   - No change needed here.

KEY CONSIDERATIONS:
- The external journal's block layout is different from an internal journal. The JBD2 superblock is at block 0 of the device, and journal blocks start immediately after. There's no inode or extent mapping.
- We need to compute JournalSegment { start, blocks } for the external device based on the JBD2 superblock's s_maxlen field.
- The replay must write to the data device, not the journal device. The existing replay infrastructure takes a BlockDevice for writes — we just need to pass the data device.
- After successful replay, we should clear the needs_recovery flag on the data device's superblock.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic data FS + external journal pair, dirties the journal, and verifies replay applies blocks to the data device.
- The log message changes from 'UUID validation only' to 'external journal replay completed'.
- UUID mismatch still fails fast.
- Block size mismatch still fails fast.
- ExternalJournalInfo is populated with journal metadata after successful replay.
- The 'required crash recovery' error path still works when no external path is provided.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:40.642853327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763994355Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763527321Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete. External journal paired replay is fully implemented: UUID validation, segment computation (bd-vdk6), and replay wiring (bd-vql1) are all done. 4 tests covering success path, UUID mismatch, dirty FS requirement, and clean FS skip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","journal","journal_dev"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vdk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:55.294301302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vql1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:56.821268230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-k9zm","title":"Apply fast commit operations to filesystem metadata during mount","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 fast commit (FC) is a lightweight journal mechanism that records individual filesystem operations (create, link, unlink, add_range, del_range, inode update) instead of full block snapshots. On mount, these operations are replayed to recover metadata state that wasn't flushed to the main journal.

CURRENT STATE: The replay_fast_commit() function in ffs-journal parses FC tag streams and extracts FcOperation variants (Create, Link, Unlink, AddRange, DelRange, Inode). The mount-time path (maybe_collect_ext4_fast_commit_evidence in ffs-core) reads these operations and stores them as Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence — purely observational.

FEATURE_PARITY.md honestly states: 'FrankenFS still relies on full JBD2 replay for authoritative metadata recovery; FC operations are observed and reported, not yet applied to filesystem state during mount.'

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:
1. After full JBD2 replay, check if the journal has FC blocks (s_num_fc_blocks > 0 in JBD2 superblock).
2. Parse the FC tag stream from the journal's FC tail region.
3. For each committed FC transaction (HEAD...TAIL pair):
   a. Create: allocate inode, add dir entry to parent.
   b. Link: add dir entry to parent (inode already exists).
   c. Unlink: remove dir entry from parent.
   d. AddRange: insert extent mapping for the inode.
   e. DelRange: remove extent mapping range.
   f. Inode: update inode metadata (size, timestamps, etc.).
4. Write the updated metadata blocks to disk.
5. Clear the FC area in the journal superblock.

COMPLEXITY: High. This requires wiring FC operations into the same mutation paths used by the FUSE write layer (ext4_create, ext4_add_dir_entry, ext4_unlink, extent allocation, inode update). The FC operations are at a higher level of abstraction than JBD2 block replay.

KEY RISKS:
- FC operations may reference inodes that don't exist yet (the create hadn't been flushed). Need to handle this gracefully.
- FC operations may conflict with JBD2 replay results (JBD2 replayed a partial state, FC completes it). Need idempotent application.
- Truncated FC streams must fall back to full JBD2 replay only (the existing fallback behavior is correct).

DEPENDENCIES:
- Requires the fast commit dentry parsing fix (commit 6e241b0) which corrected the ext4_fc_dentry_info struct layout.
- Should be done after i_version support (bd-pzc6) since FC inode updates may need to bump version.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic image with a committed FC transaction (HEAD + Create + TAIL), mounts it, and verifies the directory entry exists.
- A test with a truncated FC stream (no TAIL) falls back to JBD2-only replay.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to remove the 'observed and reported, not yet applied' caveat.
- Evidence logging records which FC operations were applied.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:17.638537685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.306396946Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.305935052Z","close_reason":"FC operations now processed at mount time via apply_fast_commit_operations(). Committed transactions are logged as observational evidence complementing JBD2 replay. InodeUpdate triggers verification read. Fallback to JBD2-only on truncated streams. FEATURE_PARITY.md caveat removed. Commit 6a4dd9c.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fast_commit","journal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k9zm","depends_on_id":"bd-pzc6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:38.088785279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-kdk","title":"btrfs: Implement single-device logical->physical mapping (sys_chunk only)","description":"Goal: given a logical bytenr, map it to (device_id, physical_bytenr) using only the sys_chunk_array bootstrap data.

Scope (v1):
- single-device images only
- only mapping via sys_chunk_array (enough to read the chunk tree/root tree)

Deliverables:
- Parse chunk items from sys_chunk_array.
- Implement map(logical) -> physical (with bounds checking).

Acceptance:
- Fixture test: pick known logical addresses (superblock.root, superblock.chunk_root) and verify mapping lands inside the device.
- No panics; invalid mapping returns ParseError/Format.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:42.802564278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:06:19.809203889Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:06:19.809180495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kdk","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:11.407714905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
